name: Governance-Embedded MVCC Version Tokens (GEMVT)
overview: GEMVT extends MVCC version tokens to embed governance metadata (classification, clearance requirements, jurisdiction) directly in the version identifier. This enables zone map pruning at the page level — the storage engine can skip entire pages whose governance envelope doesn't match the reader's clearance, without reading the page contents.

howItWorks.title: Token Structure
howItWorks.description: Each MVCC version token encodes: transaction ID, timestamp, and a governance fingerprint derived from the page's aggregate classification bitfield. The fingerprint is compact (32-64 bits) and summarizes the governance requirements of all entries on the page.

howItWorks.title: Zone Map Construction
howItWorks.description: Page-level zone maps are extended to include governance min/max bounds. The governance fingerprint in the version token acts as a fast pre-filter: if the reader's clearance doesn't intersect the page's governance range, the page is skipped entirely.

howItWorks.title: Pruning at Scan Time
howItWorks.description: During range scans, the query engine checks version token governance fingerprints before loading page contents. This eliminates I/O for pages the reader has no clearance to access.

howItWorks.title: CABM Integration
howItWorks.description: During B-epsilon flush (CABM), governance fingerprints are recomputed for affected pages. The fingerprint is maintained incrementally — only the flushed entries' governance bits are merged into the existing page fingerprint.

comparisons.narrative: Zone maps (min/max statistics per page) are a common optimization in columnar databases like Parquet, DuckDB, and Redshift. GEMVT extends this concept to governance metadata, which is unique to TrustDB. No other database uses access control metadata for I/O pruning — they filter after reading page contents, which wastes I/O on data the reader can't access.

competitors.approach: Zone maps on value columns for scan pruning
competitors.limitation: No governance metadata in zone maps; access control is pre-query, not per-page
Value-based only; no security metadata; read-time filtering only
Value-based range indexing; no governance integration; coarser granularity
Effective for sorted data only; no security-based pruning
